title: Processing fallopian tube tissue for single cell sequencing analysis
author: Ramlogan Sowamber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bu4znyx6

steps.title: In this step, cells isolated from...
steps.instruction: In this step, cells isolated from fallopian tube tissues in Step 1.13 are in the form of a pellet within a 15 mL conical tube and are ready to proceed onto the following steps.

steps.title: Gently re-suspend pellet with 500 uL...
steps.instruction: Gently re-suspend pellet with 500 uL of cell culture media to dissociate pellet

steps.title: Pre-wet a 5 mL polystyrene round-bottom...
steps.instruction: Pre-wet a 5 mL polystyrene round-bottom tube with cell strainer cap with 50 uL of cell culture media

steps.title: Allow liquid to flow through by...
steps.instruction: Allow liquid to flow through by gravity (if liquid does not flow through, either pipette liquid up and down to allow liquid to flow through or seal cap tube with parafilm and centrifuge tube at 300 x g for 1 min)

steps.title: Add 500 uL of resuspended cells from Step 2
steps.instruction: Add 500 uL of resuspended cells from Step 2.1 to pre-wetted cell strainer cap and allow cells to flow through by gravity into 5 mL tube

steps.title: Place parafilm on cap to prevent...
steps.instruction: Place parafilm on cap to prevent contamination when taken out of the biological safety cabinet

steps.title: In this step, cells previously frozen...
steps.instruction: In this step, cells previously frozen and kept at -80°C or liquid nitrogen from Step 1.13 can be thawed and processed

steps.title: Thaw cryo-vial rapidly into 5 mL...
steps.instruction: Thaw cryo-vial rapidly into 5 mL pre-warmed cell culture USG media.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 300 x g for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 300 x g for 5 minutes

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant

steps.title: Re-suspend cells gently in 500 uL of cell culture media
steps.instruction: Re-suspend cells gently in 500 uL of cell culture media

steps.title: Pre-wet a 5 mL polystyrene round-bottom...
steps.instruction: Pre-wet a 5 mL polystyrene round-bottom tube with cell strainer cap with 50uL of cell culture media

steps.title: Allow liquid to flow through by...
steps.instruction: Allow liquid to flow through by gravity (if liquid does not flow through, either pipette liquid up and down to allow liquid to flow through or seal cap tube with parafilm and centrifuge tube at 300 x g for 1 min)

steps.title: Add 500 uL of resuspended cells from Step 3
steps.instruction: Add 500 uL of resuspended cells from Step 3.4 to pre-wetted cell strainer cap and allow cells to flow through by gravity into 5 mL tube

steps.title: Place parafilm on cap to prevent...
steps.instruction: Place parafilm on cap to prevent contamination when taken out of the biological safety cabinet

steps.title: Place tubes with cells in an...
steps.instruction: Place tubes with cells in an incubator (37°C and 5% CO 2 ) for 15 minutes

steps.title: Proceed to Step 4
steps.instruction: Proceed to Step 4.

steps.title: Proceed to Step 4
steps.instruction: Proceed to Step 4

steps.title: This step is used for assessing...
steps.instruction: This step is used for assessing cell viability and counting the number of cells prior to sequencing

steps.title: Count cells using the Nexcelom Cellometer...
steps.instruction: Count cells using the Nexcelom Cellometer K2 or equivalent cell counter

steps.title: Assess viability and cell numbers before proceeding to sequencing
steps.instruction: Assess viability and cell numbers before proceeding to sequencing

steps.title: At this step, the required volume...
steps.instruction: At this step, the required volume of sample has been used for sequencing. If cell sample remains after single cell sequencing, proceed with the following steps.

steps.title: Prepare a 24 well Primaria plate...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 24 well Primaria plate for remaining cells by coating with 250 uL of collagen

steps.title: Aspirate excess collagen
steps.instruction: Aspirate excess collagen

steps.title: Add 250 uL of cell culture media to each well
steps.instruction: Add 250 uL of cell culture media to each well

steps.title: Remove cells from incubator (placed in incubator from Step 4
steps.instruction: Remove cells from incubator (placed in incubator from Step 4.3) and very gently re-suspend liquid containing cells

steps.title: Add cells to a labelled well...
steps.instruction: Add cells to a labelled well of the 24 well collagen coated Primaria plate using a pipette

steps.title: Gently move plate back and forth to evenly distribute cells
steps.instruction: Gently move plate back and forth to evenly distribute cells

steps.title: Place cells in incubator (37°C and...
steps.instruction: Place cells in incubator (37°C and 5% CO 2 ) and allow to grow. Primary fallopian tube cells

steps.title: Step 1
steps.instruction: Step 1.1 to 1.13 details steps for digesting human fallopian tube tissue using pronase. Fallopian tube and fimbria tissue samples

steps.title: Collect the fallopian tube tissue within two hours of removal
steps.instruction: Collect the fallopian tube tissue within two hours of removal. Samples are transported from the operating room/hospital in DMEM medium with 10% FBS (transport media) on ice

steps.title: In a biological safety cabinet with...
steps.instruction: In a biological safety cabinet with appropriate PPE, aspirate the transport medium leaving behind 5 mL of media.

steps.title: Place tissue into cell culture dish...
steps.instruction: Place tissue into cell culture dish and use blade to finely mince tissue. DO NOT USE MORTAR AND PESTLE. Meanwhile label 50 mL conical tube with identifying information. Minced fallopian tube tissue

steps.title: Using a 25 mL pipette, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a 25 mL pipette, transfer minced tissue to 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Add 15 mL of pronase solution to 50 mL tube
steps.instruction: Add 15 mL of pronase solution to 50 mL tube. Add 150 uL of DNase to 15 mL solution.

steps.title: Place 50 mL tube containing minced...
steps.instruction: Place 50 mL tube containing minced tissue on an orbital shaker at 4 °C and allow to digest for a minimum 24 hours and maximum 72 hours.

steps.title: Proceed to step 1
steps.instruction: Proceed to step 1.8 after 24 - 72 hours. DO NOT LEAVE TISSUE IN PRONASE FOR MORE THAN 72 hours.

steps.title: Remove 50 mL tube from 4...
steps.instruction: Remove 50 mL tube from 4 °C Fallopian tube tissue sample after 48 hour pronase digestion at 4°C.

steps.title: Place remaining cells in 37°C incubator...
steps.instruction: Place remaining cells in 37°C incubator until ready to proceed to Step 5. Do not leave cells in incubator for more than 10 minutes.

steps.title: Place tubes with cells in an...
steps.instruction: Place tubes with cells in an incubator (37°C and 5% CO 2 ) for 15 minutes

steps.title: If you would like to proceed...
steps.instruction: If you would like to proceed with storing the live cells, carefully remove the supernatant and re-suspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of freezing media and cryopreserve cells in a -80°C freezer (transfer to liquid nitrogen within 48 hours). The number of cryovials used for cell cryopreservation depends on the size of the tissue. Otherwise gently aspirate supernatant and proceed to Step 2.

steps.title: Add approximately 20 mL of RPMI...
steps.instruction: Add approximately 20 mL of RPMI solution to tube which should amount to no more than 40 mL of total media.

steps.title: Gently shake 50 mL tube back...
steps.instruction: Gently shake 50 mL tube back and forth to mix media

steps.title: Place 50 mL tube into a...
steps.instruction: Place 50 mL tube into a rack and allow the tissue to settle to the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Spin both tubes in centrifuge at...
steps.instruction: Spin both tubes in centrifuge at 1500 rpm for 15 minutes at 4°C
